followups.description: handoff-protocol.md lines 426-428 still allow Sage direct commits at 'standard' lock level (WARN only). These lines predate the COMMIT_DIRECTIVE protocol and now contradict it. The correct behavior is: Sage never commits directly regardless of lock level. Lines 426-428 need to be rewritten to route all Sage git operations through deployment-engineer via COMMIT_DIRECTIVE.

studio-director/agent.md line 166 reads 'commit to git (Sage owns commits)'. This characterization is now wrong — Sage authorizes commits; deployment-engineer owns the git operation. Should read: 'commit to git (Sage authorizes via COMMIT_DIRECTIVE; deployment-engineer executes)'.

COMMIT_DIRECTIVE field schema is M1-minimal. Flag the following as 'M-future required' for SOC2 audit posture: commit_signed (bool), gpg_key_id, workstream_id (currently optional in memory write but not in the directive itself). Add a schema_notes section to deployment-engineer/agent.md documenting which fields are M1-minimal vs M-future.

The recursive proof claim is technically a workaround — deployment-engineer was spawned as general-purpose with the new agent.md injected, not via the named-agent registry. This is a gap worth documenting in the deployment-engineer creation go-signal or an ADR addendum, so the audit trail is clear.
